title: henningia alberti
intro: Henningia alberti is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the Albert's boragine or Albert's hound's-tongue. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Henningia alberti include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be taken internally or applied topically to the affected area.
